item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Makes you wonder how many staff members didn't reply.

Though I have been thinking of a Tale where one of the PSM-1 members recognizes a coworker, and approaches them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They should've disregarded the memo, then.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta say, that's a really slick logo you designed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author of the personnel request (paraphrased):

I need a new serial killer to study SCP-5553. Would any stone-cold killers who have (a) lied their ways into jobs with the Foundation, and (b) covertly breached containment of SCP-5553 at some point, please contact me immediately? No cops this time, I promise.

13 different staff members:

Sure.

Author of the personnel request (paraphrased):

…PSM-1-A through PSM-1-M. I know who they are and will take their identities to my grave… …probably by the end of the week. I will sleep with an AK-47 under my pillow from now on. (Never sleeps again).

Upvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The only problem I have with this is the memo from the Director:

+ show block
– hide block

If you have a social media account on https://jack.███, please contact me immediately. Your privacy will be protected. Your "expertise" is required.

All others, please disregard this memo.

Surely this would lead to people who are not familiar with the site visiting it and experiencing the infohazard, no?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: replied in the wrong place here, disregard

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm kinda torn on this one. On one hand I like how the last line pays off the Moriarty setup, as well as to explain why the site exists, but the rest of it didn't interest me much. The idea of a forum for serial killers isn't particularly unique, and the D-Class' escape was rather dull to read. Honestly Moriarty is the most interesting part of the article alongside the anomalous nature of the site and how it would differentiate between a serial killer or not. Scrapping the escape plot and focusing more on that would make it a much more interesting article.

Novote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted halfway through the description when I realized what Special Clearance PSM-1 must be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is such a phenomenal idea and brilliantly executed. Just so, so clever. The idea of a social media site for serial killers, the casual reveal that the Foundation has at least 13 serial killers working for them in a professional capacity, the reveal at the end that
+ Spoiler
- Spoiler

The website's infohazard is how Moriarty performs their serial killings.

Just delightful.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What intrigues me here is there's a whole memetic that filters out all the information on screen from people who aren't serial killers. I would like to see an exploration of how it identifies said serial killers. Like, do they self identify as killers? Or is it anyone in the entire world who, through any means (voluntary or otherwise) have caused the deaths of at least 3 people? Would a drunk driver who caused a wreck qualify? Where do soldiers and MTF members fall?

This definitely calls for some additional tales at least

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 13 that came forward, likely more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So much for cold not cruel!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 13 of them, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really appreciate this feedback. I would be interested in revisiting the JACK community…I might make a tale about it. I'll keep you posted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The insinuation that members of the Foundation are classified as serial killers is terrifying, I love it! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is just delightful. I honestly couldn't have cared less about the D-Class escape plot, that was the least fun part of the article for me. What worked was that the culture of this serial killer online society felt fully-realized and complete. The "D-7372 admitted he had an account before his incarceration." line was great, and where I upvoted, even if the subsequent stuff with Moriarity was a bit pedestrian. The ending is another high note. What works here is fantastic enough that I didn't mind the ho-hum D-Class story, but this would be an all-time classic if that stuff was cut out in favor of something that shows off the great characterization and originality you seem to be tapping into when writing the site's motley crew of characters coming into their own separately from the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Know what's neat? the black box offset of the "JA" and "CK" pretty much eliminate this issue. Source: I used to design emails and logos until my recent layoff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: https://imgur.com/gallery/vZkKgBh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: +1, great concept and execution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great concept, great execution. The last line adds that hint of classic SCP mystery. Please, take my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… Reddit for murderers.

I like it.

Strangely for a community of murderers, it seems so… well: welcoming! solidariful! And sure, Moriarty does want D-Class to work for him forever in exchange for freeing him… but hey, you could tell Moriarty has mostly good intentions. Murderers gotta stand with each other, after all.

Though I wonder...
Moriarty...

was Moriarty the website?

Anyway, have this +1, and buy an aged bottle of Jack Daniels with it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All images are created by me, from scratch, and are released under CC-BY-SA 3.0.
